anatomy.description: Container that applies bottom border-radius based on the page background context. Uses clipsContent to enforce the rounded shape on the inner Header.
An instance of the Header component set (any variant). Fills the full width and height of the wrapper.

variants.name: bg
variants.description: Describes the background color of the page content BELOW the header. Determines whether the header has rounded bottom corners. Does not affect the header's own background color.

variantCombinations.description: Use when the page background below the header is surface/sunken. The wrapper applies 24px rounded bottom corners so the header visually floats above the recessed page content.
screenExamples: User: Detail screens with inset content

variantCombinations.description: Use when the page background below the header is surface/default (same tone as the header). No rounding — the header is flush with the page content.

sizeNote: Matches the inner Header instance — full screen width, 60–64px height.
usageContext: Always use this wrapper when placing a Header on a screen.
The bg prop must match the actual background color of the page content below the header.
notes: The bg prop refers to the page background BELOW the header, not the header's own background color.
The header's own background is always surface/neutral/default regardless of the wrapper variant.
The wrapper uses clipsContent: true to enforce the border-radius clip on the inner Header.
Both variants have a 1px bottom stroke (notification/border-default) separating header from content.
